prompt:
Last month a box lost power and our homegrown settings cache came back empty — it buffered writes in memory and flushed 'eventually'. Never again. I want a small append-only KV store where every acknowledged write is already in the log file when the call returns.

Module go-kvlog, package kvlog, this repo. API: Open(path string) (*Store, error) — creates the WAL if missing, replays it if present; (*Store).Set(key, value string) error; Get(key string) (string, bool); Delete(key string) error; Len() int (count of live keys); Compact() error; Close() error. Set/Delete/Compact after Close must return errors. Keys and values are arbitrary strings — embedded newlines, tabs, NUL bytes, unicode, multi-KB values, and the empty string as a VALUE is real data: after Set(k, "") a Get(k) returns ("", true), which is not the same as a missing key. Delete of a missing key is a quiet no-op. The store must be safe for concurrent use from multiple goroutines.

Durability rules — and be aware the tests literally copy the live WAL file to a new path mid-run (no Close) to simulate the power cut, then Open the copy:
- when Set/Delete return, the operation is fully in the file. No user-space buffering you don't flush before acknowledging.
- torn tail: a crash can chop bytes off the end of the file. Open on such a log must succeed, recover every record before the tear, and NEVER surface a half-written value — the final operation is either completely there or completely gone. Design the record format so a truncated record is detectable (the tests cut 1-3 bytes off the end and check that the last key either has its exact full value or is absent).
- after recovering a torn log the store keeps working: new Sets append and the file replays cleanly on the next Open — don't leave the partial garbage sitting in the middle of the log.
- replay applies operations in order: last Set wins, Delete removes.
- Compact() rewrites the log down to just the live state, in a way that is itself crash-safe to read back: after ~10x overwrite churn plus deletes the file must shrink to under half its pre-compaction size, state identical, still identical after reopen, and appends after compaction must survive further reopens.

store_test.go and recovery_test.go are the contract; do not touch them. go test -race -timeout 30s ./... must pass. I'd split record encoding / replay / compaction / the store API into separate files — your call, but not one monolith.
